Salmon Escapement Data Guidebook Preface 0.1 Start here 0.2 Scope note 0.3 Downloads

Salmon Escapement Data Guidebook DFO Pacific Region March 02, 2026 Preface This cookbook is designed as a practical field manual: first-timers can run a linear workflow experienced analysts can jump directly to pattern cards It reuses proven workflows from species-specific prep repositories and translates them into repeatable “if this, do this” guidance. 0.1 Start here New to CU time-series work? Start with Getting Started: Chapter 2 Need the right workflow quickly? Use Pattern Cards: Chapter 9 Need a runnable walk-through? Use Worked Examples: Chapter 10 Releasing outputs? Run Validation Checklists: Chapter 11 0.2 Scope note This guide covers common and high-value patterns. It does not attempt to codify every edge case; when a case falls outside pattern coverage, escalate to a documented custom workflow. 0.3 Downloads PDF EPUB
introduction.html

1 Introduction 1.1 What this guide is 1.2 Who this is for 1.3 What this guide is not 1.4 What you will produce 1.5 How to use this cookbook 1.6 Five-step workflow at a glance 1.7 Core principles 1.8 Policy and method anchors 1.9 Limits and escalation

1 Introduction 1.1 What this guide is A follow-me cookbook for building CU-based escapement time series for State of the Salmon and Wild Salmon Policy (WSP) workflows. This guide is intentionally practical: if you are in a known situation, you should be able to find the matching pattern and run it step-by-step. 1.2 Who this is for Biologists and analysts building CU time series for the first time Analysts who already build CU series but want reusable patterns and QA checks Reviewers who need a clear record of what was done and why 1.3 What this guide is not Not a full replacement for expert judgement Not a complete catalog of every CU edge case Not a place to reinvent existing code already used in production workflows 1.4 What you will produce CU-level time series outputs (wild/total as needed) Pop-level supporting outputs where applicable Metadata and metric-spec inputs suitable for WSP metric runs A repeatable run log and QC evidence package 1.5 How to use this cookbook 1.5.1 Lane A: First-time user (linear) Read Getting Started (Chapter 2) Follow workflow steps (Chapters 3–8) Pick the nearest Pattern Card (Chapter 9) Run a Worked Example (Chapter 10) Complete checklists (Chapter 11) 1.5.2 Lane B: Experienced user (lookup) Jump to Pattern Cards (Chapter 9) Run the matching script path Use checklists (Chapter 11) for release QC 1.6 Five-step workflow at a glance Source data verification Site/survey selection Record selection Record processing Estimation to CU-level series In parallel: complete metadata + metric specifications so the outputs can be reviewed and reused. 1.7 Core principles Reuse before rewrite: start from existing species workflows Document every exception: no hidden “magic” edits QC is part of the run: uniqueness, year coverage, schema checks Keep decisions explicit: when to apply a pattern, and when to escalate 1.8 Policy and method anchors This cookbook aligns to core WSP references and related status-assessment methods: WSP policy page: https://www.pac.dfo-mpo.gc.ca/fm-gp/salmon-saumon/wsp-pss/policy-politique-eng.html CSAS 2007/070 (Conservation Units) CSAS 2009/058 (Indicators and benchmarks) CSAS ScR 2024/004 (Rapid status approximation context) 1.9 Limits and escalation If your case does not match a pattern card (or fails QC), stop and escalate to a custom workflow with documented assumptions.
getting-started.html

2 Getting Started 2.1 Quick orientation 2.2 Repository map (what each repo is for) 2.3 Standard prep-repo folders 2.4 Package bootstrap (R) 2.5 Pre-run checklist (required) 2.6 Minimum key-file checks by species 2.7 Before moving to Step 1

2 Getting Started 2.1 Quick orientation You will use this cookbook repo plus one or more production prep repos. Think of this guide as orchestration: pattern cards tell you which pipeline to run worked examples show exact run paths checklists protect release quality 2.2 Repository map (what each repo is for) 2.2.1 Core cookbook dfo-pacific-science/cu-escapement-data-cookbook Purpose: follow-me procedures, examples, templates, QA 2.2.2 Species prep repos (primary execution) BronwynMacDonald/FRSK-WSPDataPrep Fraser Sockeye CU + pop prep (complex gap-fill logic) BronwynMacDonald/FRCo-WSPDataPrep Interior Fraser Coho prep (CU/pop + brood-year components) BronwynMacDonald/FRCm-WSPDataPrep Lower Fraser Chum prep BronwynMacDonald/FRPink-WSPDataPrep Fraser Pink prep 2.2.3 Source-report repos (method context) code/Data-Standards Source workflow framing and case-study context code/Metadata-Questionnaire-CU-Series Field definitions, metadata examples, summary patterns 2.2.4 Downstream metrics/tooling repos Pacific-salmon-assess/WSP-Metrics-Pkg Pacific-salmon-assess/WSP-Rapid-Status-WorkedExamples 2.3 Standard prep-repo folders Most species repos use: DATA_IN/ input files for annual updates DATA_LOOKUP_FILES/ lookups/crosswalks DATA_PROCESSING/ intermediates (species-dependent) DATA_OUT/ deliverable flat files DATA_TRACKING/ diagnostics and review artifacts CODE/ executable scripts 2.4 Package bootstrap (R) Install baseline packages before first run: install.packages(c( "dplyr", "tidyr", "stringr", "forcats", "Hmisc", "mgsub", "tidyverse" )) Note: some pipelines can run with a subset, but Sockeye/Coho scripts often assume the broader set. 2.5 Pre-run checklist (required) Confirm repo access for all required species pipelines Confirm latest annual files are staged in DATA_IN/ Confirm lookup files/crosswalks are current Confirm target run year and release label Select the matching Pattern Card (Chapter 9) Open a run log (Appendix template) 2.6 Minimum key-file checks by species Sockeye: SKAll, SR file, CU stream maps, expansion-year table Coho: latest IFC data extract, infill exceptions, CU info + POPID lookup Chum: major systems, Harrison summary, extensive pivot, POPID crosswalk Pink: official esc-rec CU file + historical NuSEDS source file 2.7 Before moving to Step 1 You should have: selected pipeline(s) staged inputs a destination release folder/branch named reviewer(s) for QC sign-off
step-1-source-data-verification.html

3 Step 1: Source Data Verification 3.1 Objective 3.2 Do this exactly 3.3 Quick validation script (adapt path as needed) 3.4 Species-specific verification notes 3.5 Required outputs from Step 1 3.6 Escalate when

3 Step 1: Source Data Verification 3.1 Objective Confirm that inputs are current, identifiable, and interpretable before any selection or estimation logic. 3.2 Do this exactly Build an input inventory (file name, source contact, pull date, run year). Validate core identifiers: CU IDs Pop/site IDs year fields species/run-timing fields where applicable Confirm unit meaning: wild vs total expanded vs observed biological zero vs missing Record anomalies and owner for resolution. 3.3 Quick validation script (adapt path as needed) x <- read.csv("DATA_IN/<input-file>.csv") # Required columns (edit per file) required <- c("Year") stopifnot(all(required %in% names(x))) # Year sanity stopifnot(!all(is.na(x$Year))) # Duplicate check on candidate key (edit key fields) key <- c("Year") if (all(key %in% names(x))) { stopifnot(!any(duplicated(x[key]))) } 3.4 Species-specific verification notes Sockeye: verify timing labels and stream-name consistency against decoder and CU stream lists. Coho: verify tributary naming against POPID lookup before final joins. Chum: verify Harrison + Major systems year alignment before aggregation. Pink: verify official esc-rec year coverage and historical NuSEDS alignment. 3.5 Required outputs from Step 1 source-inventory.csv (or markdown equivalent) verification-notes.md with anomalies + decisions list of files approved for Step 2 3.6 Escalate when IDs are missing for high-priority records source year ranges conflict across required files a field meaning changed from prior year without documentation
step-2-site-and-survey-selection.html

4 Step 2: Site and Survey Selection 4.1 Objective 4.2 Do this exactly 4.3 Minimum rule set to document 4.4 Practical implementation notes from existing repos 4.5 Suggested output artifacts 4.6 Optional helper (from Data-Standards repo) 4.7 Escalate when

4 Step 2: Site and Survey Selection 4.1 Objective Build a transparent inclusion/exclusion set of sites/surveys for each CU. 4.2 Do this exactly Start from the verified lookup/crosswalk tables. Apply inclusion rules (quality, verification state, enhancement policy). Apply exclusion rules (aggregation duplicates, data-deficient records, explicit policy exclusions). Export machine-readable include/exclude lists. Export plain-language summary text for metadata. 4.3 Minimum rule set to document quality threshold used enhancement handling rule aggregation/double-counting rule special-case handling rule 4.4 Practical implementation notes from existing repos Sockeye: selection is tightly coupled to stream maps and run timing; keep decoder + stream lists synchronized. Coho: tributary names must align to POPID crosswalk for clean output joins. Chum/Pink: selection is simpler, but source table structure still needs year and naming consistency checks. 4.5 Suggested output artifacts site_selection_include.csv site_selection_exclude.csv site_selection_summary.md (human-readable rationale) 4.6 Optional helper (from Data-Standards repo) The source technical workflow includes helper patterns for generated site-selection narrative text: code/Data-Standards/FUNCTIONS/FUNCTION_extract_SiteSelectionText.R 4.7 Escalate when site counts change sharply from prior release without clear cause key systems disappear due to naming or lookup drift inclusion rules conflict across source datasets
step-3-record-selection.html

5 Step 3: Record Selection 5.1 Objective 5.2 Do this exactly 5.3 Recommended hierarchy template 5.4 Mandatory checks 5.5 Example implementation patterns 5.6 Required outputs from Step 3 5.7 Escalate when

5 Step 3: Record Selection 5.1 Objective Select the single best usable record per analysis key (typically Pop/CU + Year) before processing adjustments. 5.2 Do this exactly Define a deterministic selection hierarchy. Apply it consistently. Capture all overrides in a decision log. 5.3 Recommended hierarchy template preferred estimate class/type preferred survey method/platform preferred verified source manual expert override (must be documented) 5.4 Mandatory checks no duplicate selected rows by key zero-policy applied consistently (biological zero vs missing) year assignment rule is explicit (UseYear or equivalent) 5.5 Example implementation patterns Coho workflow can be configured to use WSP-only systems vs broader set; document your setting in the run log. Sockeye workflow uses run-timing-aware matching and custom handling for known problematic stream/year combinations; keep these choices explicit. 5.6 Required outputs from Step 3 selected_records.csv selection_decisions.md selection_exclusions.csv 5.7 Escalate when multiple records tie after full hierarchy estimate-class shifts cause large retrospective changes zero/missing ambiguity cannot be resolved from source documentation
step-4-record-processing.html

6 Step 4: Record Processing 6.1 Objective 6.2 Processing actions (in order) 6.3 Species-pattern notes from current production repos 6.4 Minimum output columns (recommended) 6.5 Required outputs from Step 4 6.6 Escalate when

6 Step 4: Record Processing 6.1 Objective Transform selected records into analysis-ready values with explicit adjustment flags. 6.2 Processing actions (in order) Apply approved infill/expansion logic (if applicable). Apply enhancement handling rules (wild vs total expectations). Harmonize year fields and numeric formats. Preserve both raw and adjusted values. Emit flags for every adjustment type. 6.3 Species-pattern notes from current production repos Sockeye: multiple CU-specific gap-fill functions exist (regular, Quesnel, Shuswap, Takla-Trem, average-based); treat these as declared methods, not hidden code behavior. Coho: decomposition between hatchery and natural-origin components feeds CU brood-year outputs; verify assumptions remain unchanged. Chum: CU totals are assembled from major systems + Harrison components; document composition assumptions. Pink: official CU series is retained while historical stream context is merged for pop-level representation. 6.4 Minimum output columns (recommended) key IDs (CU/Pop/year) processed value(s) raw value(s) adjustment flags (infill/scale/enhancement) notes or method tag 6.5 Required outputs from Step 4 processed_records.csv processing_log.md 6.6 Escalate when adjustments materially change trend interpretation but method justification is weak custom manual fixes are introduced without reproducible rule expression
step-5-estimation.html
7 Step 5: Estimation 7.1 Objective 7.2 Choose estimation mode 7.3 Do this exactly 7.4 Minimum estimation QC 7.5 Downstream metric execution 7.6 Required outputs from Step 5 7.7 Escalate when

7 Step 5: Estimation 7.1 Objective Produce final CU-level time series suitable for WSP status workflows and metric execution. 7.2 Choose estimation mode Direct sum: complete/near-complete site coverage with stable inclusion rules Indicator expansion: partial coverage with defensible expansion factors Run reconstruction/model-based: where aggregate + stock-share or modeled structure is required 7.3 Do this exactly generate CU series from processed records apply start-year policy where defined attach benchmark/metric-ready fields compare against prior release and explain differences 7.4 Minimum estimation QC uniqueness by (CU_ID, Year) plausible continuity (no unexplained structural breaks) expected missingness pattern documented trend/abundance fields aligned with intended metric usage 7.5 Downstream metric execution Preferred tooling: https://github.com/Pacific-salmon-assess/WSP-Metrics-Pkg https://github.com/Pacific-salmon-assess/WSP-Rapid-Status-WorkedExamples 7.6 Required outputs from Step 5 Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_<Species>.csv release_diff_summary.md metric-input-ready table(s) or mapping note 7.7 Escalate when prior-release diffs are large and unexplained benchmark assumptions differ from metric configuration estimation mode changed relative to prior release
step-6-metadata-and-metric-specifications.html

8 Step 6: Metadata and Metric Specifications 8.1 Objective 8.2 Metadata package components 8.3 How-to-fill blocks 8.4 Practical source files for metadata definitions 8.5 Minimum validation script 8.6 Required outputs from Step 6

8 Step 6: Metadata and Metric Specifications 8.1 Objective Produce a metadata package that makes each CU time series understandable, reviewable, and machine-usable. This chapter follows the practical conversion approach from the metadata questionnaire distillation: convert question prompts into how-to-fill procedures. 8.2 Metadata package components At minimum, keep these files together: metadata_cu.csv metric_specs.csv codes.csv (controlled vocabularies) metadata_notes.md 8.3 How-to-fill blocks 8.3.1 1) CU context Fields to fill: CU_ID, CU_Name, Species verification status + year geographic/life-history context fields used by your program Rules: keep CU ID format stable across all files if aliases exist, document mapping in a lookup field/table 8.3.2 2) Data source + calculation method Fields to fill: source system(s) source pull date(s) year coverage spawner estimate type and calculation method Rules: describe method in plain language plus coded value include file/script provenance for reproducibility 8.3.3 3) Benchmarks Fields to fill: benchmark type(s) method reference benchmark values and units Rules: include citations or report references for benchmark method distinguish operational benchmark from reference-only benchmark 8.3.4 4) Active interventions Fields to fill: hatchery/spawning channel influence flags adjustment notes where interventions affect interpretation 8.3.5 5) Metric specifications Fields to fill: enabled metrics by CU metric parameters cycle/benchmark settings where relevant Rules: align column names and coding with the metric engine inputs you will run 8.3.6 6) Assessment process + provenance Fields to fill: reviewers/assessment process decision date run identifier script + repo version 8.4 Practical source files for metadata definitions Local method and field-definition context is available in: code/Metadata-Questionnaire-CU-Series/REPORT/02_questionnaire.Rmd code/Metadata-Questionnaire-CU-Series/DATA/From_Google_Sheet/Metadata_FieldDefinitions.csv code/Metadata-Questionnaire-CU-Series/DATA/From_Google_Sheet/Metadata_Questions.csv Use these to avoid inventing inconsistent labels. 8.5 Minimum validation script m <- read.csv("metadata_cu.csv") req <- c("CU_ID", "CU_Name", "Species") stopifnot(all(req %in% names(m))) stopifnot(!any(is.na(m$CU_ID) | m$CU_ID == "")) 8.6 Required outputs from Step 6 completed metadata + metric specs controlled vocabulary file short note documenting unresolved fields and owners

9 Patterns and Templates 9.1 How to use pattern cards 9.2 Pattern Card 1 — Fraser Sockeye (direct sum + targeted gap fills) 9.3 Pattern Card 2 — Interior Fraser Coho (CU decomposition + brood-year outputs) 9.4 Pattern Card 3 — Lower Fraser Chum (major-system aggregate) 9.5 Pattern Card 4 — Fraser Pink (official CU series + historical stream layer) 9.6 Template bundle (use with any pattern)

9 Patterns and Templates 9.1 How to use pattern cards Treat each card as a do-this-exactly runbook for a common scenario. Each card includes: when to use it required inputs exact run steps expected outputs minimum QC checks escalation triggers 9.2 Pattern Card 1 — Fraser Sockeye (direct sum + targeted gap fills) 9.2.1 Use when Species is Fraser Sockeye You need CU and pop flat files aligned with existing SoS/WSP practice You need built-in handling for known gap-fill pathways (e.g., Quesnel, Shuswap, Takla-Trem, Nahatlatch) 9.2.2 Repo and script Repo: FRSK-WSPDataPrep Script: CODE/Prep4_CleanedFlatFile_Fraser_Sk_KDEdits2024.R 9.2.3 Required inputs DATA_IN/SKAll (Nov YYYY).csv DATA_IN/Fraser Sockeye SR Data_YYYY.csv DATA_IN/Sockeye_Fraser_CU_Streams.csv DATA_IN/Sockeye_Fraser_SR_Streams.csv DATA_IN/Sockeye_Fraser_Expansion_Years.csv DATA_LOOKUP_FILES/MOD_MAIN_CU_LOOKUP_FOR_SOS.csv DATA_LOOKUP_FILES/Site_Info_GeneratedDecoder_Fraser_Sk.csv 9.2.4 Run cd FRSK-WSPDataPrep Rscript CODE/Prep4_CleanedFlatFile_Fraser_Sk_KDEdits2024.R 9.2.5 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserSockeye.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserSockeye.csv DATA_TRACKING/FraserSockeye_MatchingCheck.csv DATA_TRACKING/Sites not in CUs.csv 9.2.6 Minimum QC CU-level uniqueness by (CU_ID, Year) Year range reaches current update year Sites not in CUs.csv reviewed and signed off Manual exception years (e.g., Taseko/Birkenhead) documented in run log 9.2.7 Escalate if CU totals fail plausibility checks against prior release input stream naming changes break decoder joins new missingness patterns appear in high-priority CUs 9.3 Pattern Card 2 — Interior Fraser Coho (CU decomposition + brood-year outputs) 9.3.1 Use when Species is Interior Fraser Coho You need CU and pop outputs plus hatchery/natural decomposition flow 9.3.2 Repo and scripts Repo: FRCo-WSPDataPrep Scripts: CODE/SUB_Data extraction and clean up (Coho for now)_2023.R CODE/Prep5_CleanedFlatFile_Fraser_Coho.R 9.3.3 Required inputs latest DATA_IN/IFC Data 1975-<year> ... for Salmon Scanner.csv DATA_IN/IFC Infill exceptions.csv DATA_IN/FRSK_CU_Info_masterUpdate.csv DATA_IN/Fraser Coho POPID Lookup...csv DATA_LOOKUP_FILES/MOD_MAIN_CU_LOOKUP_FOR_SOS.csv 9.3.4 Run cd FRCo-WSPDataPrep Rscript CODE/Prep5_CleanedFlatFile_Fraser_Coho.R 9.3.5 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserCoho.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserCoho.csv DATA_PROCESSING/All IFC CUs BY Table_EC.max=NA_infill=FALSE.csv 9.3.6 Minimum QC CU output starts at expected analysis years (typically 1998+) CU-level uniqueness by (CU_ID, Year) Pop-level Pop_ID coverage reviewed for unmatched names Recruit columns checked for expected NA windows in terminal years 9.3.7 Escalate if large shifts in hatchery vs natural components are unexplained key tributary naming/crosswalks no longer match lookup rules 9.4 Pattern Card 3 — Lower Fraser Chum (major-system aggregate) 9.4.1 Use when Species is Lower Fraser Chum You need fast CU + pop outputs using major systems + extensive pivot 9.4.2 Repo and script Repo: FRCm-WSPDataPrep Script: CODE/Prep7_Create Fraser Chum2.R 9.4.3 Required inputs DATA_IN/FR Chum Major Systems.csv DATA_IN/Harrison Chum Escapement Summary.csv DATA_IN/Chum extensive pivot.csv DATA_IN/FR Chum POPID Crosswalk.csv 9.4.4 Run cd FRCm-WSPDataPrep Rscript "CODE/Prep7_Create Fraser Chum2.R" 9.4.5 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserChum.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserChum_all.csv 9.4.6 Minimum QC CU year range aligns with known truncation (2002+ in current implementation) remove unnamed first column if present before downstream use pop-level row counts/missingness reviewed against prior release 9.5 Pattern Card 4 — Fraser Pink (official CU series + historical stream layer) 9.5.1 Use when Species is Fraser Pink You need CU series from official esc-rec source plus pop-level historical stream representation 9.5.2 Repo and script Repo: FRPink-WSPDataPrep Script: CODE/Prep6_Create Fraser Pink_2.R 9.5.3 Required inputs DATA_IN/Fraser Pink esc_rec CU.csv DATA_IN/FR_Pink_Conservation_Unit_Data_NUSEDs_June 2023.csv 9.5.4 Run cd FRPink-WSPDataPrep Rscript "CODE/Prep6_Create Fraser Pink_2.R" 9.5.5 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserPink.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserPink_All_Nuseds.csv 9.5.6 Minimum QC CU years include latest run year remove unnamed first column if present before downstream use historical years and mainstem representation are both present in pop output expected NA behavior in trend/rec columns reviewed 9.6 Template bundle (use with any pattern) For each run, store: run-log.md — what you ran, when, with which files decision-log.md — any deviations/exceptions and why qc-summary.md — pass/fail checks with evidence links

10 Worked Examples 10.1 Worked Example A — Fraser Sockeye annual update 10.2 Worked Example B — Interior Fraser Coho annual update 10.3 Worked Example C — Lower Fraser Chum annual update 10.4 Worked Example D — Fraser Pink annual update

10 Worked Examples This chapter gives concrete, reproducible runs you can follow directly. 10.1 Worked Example A — Fraser Sockeye annual update 10.1.1 Scenario You received updated annual Sockeye files and need refreshed CU and pop outputs. 10.1.2 Run cd FRSK-WSPDataPrep Rscript CODE/Prep4_CleanedFlatFile_Fraser_Sk_KDEdits2024.R 10.1.3 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserSockeye.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserSockeye.csv tracking artifacts in DATA_TRACKING/ 10.1.4 Minimum QC cu <- read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserSockeye.csv") stopifnot(!any(duplicated(cu[c("CU_ID", "Year")]))) pop <- read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserSockeye.csv") stopifnot(all(c("Pop_ID", "CU_ID", "Year") %in% names(pop))) 10.1.5 Notes Document any custom gap-fill/suppression years used in this run. 10.2 Worked Example B — Interior Fraser Coho annual update 10.2.1 Scenario You need updated Coho CU/pop outputs and brood-year-derived CU components. 10.2.2 Run cd FRCo-WSPDataPrep Rscript CODE/Prep5_CleanedFlatFile_Fraser_Coho.R 10.2.3 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserCoho.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserCoho.csv DATA_PROCESSING/All IFC CUs BY Table_EC.max=NA_infill=FALSE.csv 10.2.4 Minimum QC cu <- read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserCoho.csv") stopifnot(!any(duplicated(cu[c("CU_ID", "Year")]))) stopifnot(min(cu$Year, na.rm = TRUE) >= 1998) pop <- read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserCoho.csv") stopifnot(all(c("Pop_ID", "CU_ID", "Year", "Pop_Name") %in% names(pop))) 10.2.5 Notes Review tributary naming mismatches before release packaging. 10.3 Worked Example C — Lower Fraser Chum annual update 10.3.1 Scenario You need current CU and pop outputs using major-systems and extensive-table inputs. 10.3.2 Run cd FRCm-WSPDataPrep Rscript "CODE/Prep7_Create Fraser Chum2.R" 10.3.3 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserChum.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserChum_all.csv 10.3.4 Minimum QC drop_unnamed <- function(d) d[, names(d) != "", drop = FALSE] cu <- drop_unnamed(read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserChum.csv")) stopifnot(!any(duplicated(cu[c("CU_ID", "Year")]))) pop <- drop_unnamed(read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserChum_all.csv")) stopifnot(all(c("Pop_ID", "Year") %in% names(pop))) 10.3.5 Notes Current implementation is truncated to years consistent with available source composition (review annual range each update). 10.4 Worked Example D — Fraser Pink annual update 10.4.1 Scenario You need updated CU series from official esc-rec source plus pop-level historical context. 10.4.2 Run cd FRPink-WSPDataPrep Rscript "CODE/Prep6_Create Fraser Pink_2.R" 10.4.3 Expected outputs DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserPink.csv DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserPink_All_Nuseds.csv 10.4.4 Minimum QC drop_unnamed <- function(d) d[, names(d) != "", drop = FALSE] cu <- drop_unnamed(read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByCU_FraserPink.csv")) stopifnot(!any(duplicated(cu[c("CU_ID", "Year")]))) pop <- drop_unnamed(read.csv("DATA_OUT/Cleaned_FlatFile_ByPop_FraserPink_All_Nuseds.csv")) stopifnot(all(c("Pop_ID", "CU_ID", "Year") %in% names(pop))) 10.4.5 Notes Confirm expected historical + mainstem representation before release sign-off.
checklists-and-decision-trees.html

11 Checklists and Decision Trees 11.1 Annual release checklist 11.2 Pop-level key guidance (important) 11.3 Decision tree: choose estimation pattern 11.4 Decision tree: infill vs no infill 11.5 Top failure modes and fixes 11.6 Minimum reusable QC snippet

11 Checklists and Decision Trees 11.1 Annual release checklist 11.1.1 A. Data + run control latest annual inputs staged and logged pipeline + script version recorded run log + decision log initialized 11.1.2 B. Output integrity CU output exists and key uniqueness passes (CU_ID, Year) Pop output exists and key checks pass for the selected schema required core columns are present expected year range is present 11.1.3 C. Consistency checks prior-release diff reviewed and explained major missingness changes explained manual overrides/exceptions documented 11.1.4 D. Metadata + metrics readiness metadata package updated (metadata_cu, metric_specs, codes) metric-input fields align with selected data series unresolved issues assigned owner + follow-up date 11.2 Pop-level key guidance (important) Do not assume a single universal pop key across species. Sockeye/Coho may have repeated Pop_ID across names or contexts. Use a composite key appropriate to pipeline output (e.g., CU_ID + Pop_Name + Year) unless your species method defines otherwise. 11.3 Decision tree: choose estimation pattern Start: Do you have near-complete direct site coverage with stable inclusion? Yes → Direct-sum pattern No → continue Do you have defensible expansion factors from indicator systems? Yes → Indicator expansion pattern No → continue Do you have model/run-reconstruction inputs and method support? Yes → Run reconstruction/model-based pattern No → Escalate to custom method design 11.4 Decision tree: infill vs no infill Is infill policy already approved for this CU/species path? No → do not infill; escalate for policy decision Yes → continue Are infilled years and method documented and reproducible? No → stop and document before release Yes → apply and flag affected rows 11.5 Top failure modes and fixes ID mismatch across files Fix: update/confirm crosswalk, rerun joins, recheck keys. Unexpected year truncation Fix: inspect start-year rules and source file coverage. Large retrospective shifts Fix: compare input changes vs method changes; isolate source of delta. Schema drift in output columns Fix: run required-column checks and normalize structure. Unnamed first column from CSV row names Fix: drop blank-column headers before downstream processing. 11.6 Minimum reusable QC snippet x <- read.csv("DATA_OUT/<cu-file>.csv") if ("" %in% names(x)) x <- x[, names(x) != "", drop = FALSE] stopifnot(!any(duplicated(x[c("CU_ID", "Year")]))) req <- c("CU_ID", "CU_Name", "Year", "SpnForAbd_Total", "SpnForTrend_Total") stopifnot(all(req %in% names(x)))
appendix.html

12 Appendix 12.1 A. Minimal run-log template 12.2 B. Minimal decision-log template 12.3 C. Minimal QC summary template 12.4 D. Key reference links 12.5 E. Local method-context repos (for deeper reading)

12 Appendix 12.1 A. Minimal run-log template # Run Log — <species> — <run date> ## Inputs - Source files used: - Data pull dates: - Lookup/crosswalk versions: ## Commands run 1. 2. ## Outputs generated - CU file: - Pop file: - Tracking files: ## Notes - Deviations from standard pattern: - Open issues: 12.2 B. Minimal decision-log template # Decision Log — <species> — <run date> ## Decision ## Why this decision was needed ## Options considered - Option 1: - Option 2: ## Chosen approach ## Impact on outputs/QC 12.3 C. Minimal QC summary template # QC Summary — <species> — <run date> ## Required checks - [ ] CU uniqueness `(CU_ID, Year)` - [ ] Pop key checks - [ ] Year coverage check - [ ] Missingness review on core fields - [ ] Prior-release diff reviewed ## Findings ## Pass/fail ## Reviewer 12.4 D.
